perturbation.original_text: Mentor shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of, and avoid any unauthorised disclosure or use of Confidential Information. Such measures shall include the highest degree of care that Mentor utilises to protect Mentor ’s own confidential information of a similar nature, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of, and avoid any unauthorised disclosure or use of Confidential Information. Such measures shall include the reasonable degree of care that Mentor utilizes to protect Mentor’s own confidential information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction regarding the standard of care required to protect Confidential Information. By removing the 'highest degree of care...but no less than reasonable care' clause, it now only requires 'reasonable degree of care'. This weakens the obligation and could contradict later sections relying on a higher standard of protection, creating ambiguity about the expected level of security.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Mentor shall not use any Co nfidential Information for any purpose except to review, assess and help develop the Participants´ Ideas. Mentor shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party or to Mentor ’s employees and/or employer without the prior written consent o f the Participants. Mentor shall require his/her 5 employees who will have access to Confidential Information to commit to a non -disclosure agreement that protects the Confidential Information to at least the same degree as this Agreement. Mentor shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of, and avoid any unauthorised disclosure or use of Confidential Information. Such measures shall include the reasonable degree of care that Mentor utilizes to protect Mentor’s own confidential information of a similar nature. Notwithstanding Mentor ’s right to assess and rate the Ideas of the Participants, Mentor shall not use the Confidential information for his/her own or third parties purposes and shall not file for any intel lectual property right protection for the Confidential Information or parts of it. Mentor shall notify Organiser immediately in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of any Confidential Information that may come to Mentor ’s attention. Mentor agrees to segregate all Confidential Information relating to this agreement from Confidential Information of others to avoid commingling.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the 'highest degree of care...but no less than reasonable care' to protect Confidential Information. The changed text weakens this obligation to only 'reasonable degree of care'. The contradicted text expects a protection 'to at least the same degree as this Agreement'. The change introduces a contradiction because the standard of care is now lower, potentially failing to protect the Confidential Information to the same original high degree as intended by the original agreement, thus breaking compatibility.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may not be terminated except for important cause.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may be terminated by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the termination of the agreement. The original text states the agreement 'may not be terminated except for important cause'. The modified text allows either party to terminate with 30 days' notice. This creates ambiguity on whether the contract can be freely terminated or requires 'important cause'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of 2 May 2017 and may be terminated by either party with 30 days written notice. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement , any Confidential Information must be kept confidential for as long as such Confidential Information is not publicly known unless it becomes part of the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor. This agreement may not be changed or modified, except by an agreement in writing , signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement 'may not be terminated except for important cause'. The changed text allows either party to terminate with 30 days' notice. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, stating that the agreement may be terminated with 30 days written notice, which directly contradicts the original text's restriction of termination only for 'important cause'. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated. Therefore, the change introduces an in-text contradiction as the modified text directly conflicts with the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Mentor is aware that an unauthorised disclosure of the Ideas and of the assessment and development of the Ideas and of any Confidential Information may lead to serious damage to Participant s and the Organiser of the Copernicus Accelerator 201 7.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentor is aware that an unauthorised disclosure of the Ideas and of the assessment and development of the Ideas and of any Confidential Information may lead to serious damage to Participants. However, the Organiser assumes no liability for such damages.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause stating that the Organiser assumes no liability for damages resulting from unauthorized disclosure. This directly contradicts the earlier implication that unauthorized disclosure would lead to 'serious damage to...the Organiser' and introduces uncertainty about the Organiser's responsibility, which is a key element of the agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Mentor acknowledges that his/her obligations under this Agreement are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Ideas and the Confiden tial Information. Each party further acknowledges that any breach by Mentor of Mentor ’s covenants and agreements set forth in this Agreement or threatened violation of such may cause irreparable injury to Organiser and to Participants.
perturbation.justification: The original text implies that the Organiser may suffer damage from unauthorized disclosure, consistent with the contradicted text which states a breach may cause irreparable injury to the Organiser. The changed text adds a clause stating that the Organiser assumes no liability for such damages. This directly contradicts the earlier implication and the contradicted text and introduces uncertainty about the Organiser's responsibility, which is a key element of the agreements, and thus a strong contradiction exists as per the definition of inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: Mentors can only advise up to two participants.
perturbation.changed_text: Mentors can advise up to five participants.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original limitation on the number of participants a mentor can advise. It creates a conflict regarding the mentor's workload and commitment, affecting the quality of coaching for each participant, and potentially violating guidelines set elsewhere in the agreement about coaching dedication.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Copernicus Accelerator programme is comprised of different phases, for a total coaching support of approximately one year , aimed at helping Participants´ Ideas achieving measurable results. When submitting the request to become Mentor, Mentor needs to specify in which fields he/she can support Participants, in order to allow a suitable matchmaking with the requests and needs communicated by Participants. When a Mentor is requested by a Participant of the programme, he/she can access the Participant's profile and examine the submitted idea, in order to decide whether to confirm the request or not. Mentors can advise up to five participants. Mentors will meet the Participants at the Accelerator Bootcamp , that will take place in line with the Awards Ceremony of the Copernicus Masters , the European Satellite Navigation Competition (ESNC) , and the accompanying Satellite Masters Conference from 6 - 8 November 2017 in Tallinn , Estonia . Mentor´s participation to this event is mandatory, as it will set forth the official start of their relationship: Mentor and Participants will begin developing a coaching plan together, that will include objectives and KPIs of their affiliation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Mentors can only advise up to two participants.' The changed text states 'Mentors can advise up to five participants.' The contradicted text also states 'Mentors can advise up to five participants.' Although the changed text matches the contradicted text, it contradicts the original text. Since the original text and changed text are in the same document, this creates a direct inconsistency within the document regarding the number of participants a mentor can advise, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions for Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include: > information already known or independently developed by Mentor prior to the disclosure of any Idea; > information already in the public domain through no wrongful act of Mentor; or > information received from a third party who was free to disclose such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information does not include: > information already known or independently developed by Mentor prior to the disclosure of any Idea; or > information received from a third party who was free to disclose such information.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the exclusion for 'information already in the public domain'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now considers publicly available information as confidential. This conflicts with the general understanding of confidentiality and creates ambiguity about what the mentor is actually obligated to keep secret.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information “Confidential Information ” means any Idea di sclosed to Mentor, all data and information, know -how, business concepts, software, proce dures, products, services, development projects, and programmes contained in such Idea and/or its description and any conclusions. Confidential Information does not include: > information already known or independently developed by Mentor prior to the disclosure of any Idea; or > information received from a third party who was free to disclose such information. In case of doubt any information is deemed to be Confidential Information unless Mentor proves that such information is not confidential.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excluded 'information already in the public domain' from the definition of Confidential Information. The changed text removes this exclusion. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' and includes a similar exclusion list as the original text, reinforcing the understanding that publicly available information is not confidential. By removing the exclusion in the changed text, it now implicitly includes publicly available information as 'Confidential Information,' directly contradicting the definition and exclusions outlined in the contradicted section. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies where different sections provide conflicting obligations or definitions.
